name: cynthion

metadata.name: cynthion
classifiers: Topic :: System :: Hardware :: Universal Serial Bus (USB)
metadata.summary: Python package and utilities for the Great Scott Gadgets Cynthion USB Test Instrument

documentation:
# Cynthion Python Package and Utilities

This software is developed for python3.

## Installing or Updating Software

Unless you are developing software or testing software from git, you can
install or update Cynthion software with:
```
pip3 install --upgrade cynthion
```

modules.name: cynthion.errors
documentation: This file is part of Cynthion

functions.name: cynthion.errors.from_Cynthion_error
documentation: Returns the error class appropriate for the given Cynthion error.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.DeviceNotFoundError
documentation: Error indicating no Cynthion device was found.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.DeviceBusyError
documentation: Error indicating the Cynthion is too busy to service the given request.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.DeviceMemoryError
documentation: Error indicating that the Cynthion has run out of memory.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.NotFoundError
documentation: Error indicating that a resource was not found.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.CynthionError
documentation: Runtime error used when no better description is available.

classes.name: cynthion.errors.ExternalDeviceError
documentation:
Error used when a external device (e.g. not on the Cynthion)
experiences an issue. This typically means that the error is not with
the Cynthion hardware or software, but may be with e.g. connections.

SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause

classes.name: cynthion.gateware.analyzer.analyzer.USBAnalyzer

methods.name: cynthion.gateware.analyzer.analyzer.USBAnalyzer.__init__
documentation:
Parameters:
    utmi_interface -- A record or elaboratable that presents a UTMI interface.

Core USB analyzer; backed by a small ringbuffer in FPGA block RAM.

If you're looking to instantiate a full analyzer, you'll probably want to grab
one of the DRAM-based ringbuffer variants (which are currently forthcoming).

If you're looking to use this with a ULPI PHY, rather than the FPGA-convenient UTMI interface,
grab the UTMITranslator from `luna.gateware.interface.ulpi`.

# Attributes

stream: StreamInterface(), output stream

: Stream that carries USB analyzer data.

idle: Signal(), output

: Asserted iff the analyzer is not currently receiving data.

stopped: Signal(), output

: Asserted iff the analyzer is stopped and not capturing packets.

overrun: Signal(), output

: Asserted iff the analyzer has received more data than it can store in its internal buffer.
  Occurs if :attr:`stream` is not being read quickly enough.

capturing: Signal(), output

: Asserted iff the analyzer is currently capturing a packet.

discarding: Signal(), output

: Asserted iff the analyzer is discarding the contents of its internal buffer.

# Parameters

utmi_interface: UTMIInterface()

: The UTMI interface that carries the data to be analyzed.

mem_depth: int, default=8192

: The depth of the analyzer's local ringbuffer, in bytes.
  Must be a power of 2.

documentation: Adds GPIO pin definitions to the board's main GPIO object.

methods.name: cynthion.board.CynthionBoard._add_interface
documentation:
Adds a peripheral to the Cynthion object. Prefer this over adding attributes directly,
as it adds peripherals to a list that can be queried by the user.

Arguments:
    name -- The name of the attribute to add to this board. "i2c" would create a
        .i2c property on this board.
    instance -- The object to add as that property.

Arguments:

class_variables.name: cynthion.board.CynthionBoard.BOARD_PRODUCT_ID
documentation:
The mappings from GPIO names to port numbers. Paths in names can be delineated
with underscores to group gpios. For example, if Jumper 7, Pin 3 is Port 5, Pin 11,
you could add an entry that reads "J7_P3": (5, 11).
Class describing Cynthion devices.

modules.name: cynthion.cynthion
documentation: This file is part of Cynthion

functions.name: cynthion.cynthion.Cynthion
documentation:
Attempts to create a new instance of Cynthion board (sub)class
most applicable to the given device. For example, if the attached
board is a Cynthion, this will automatically create a
Cynthion object.

Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.find() method, allowing narrowing
to a more specific Cynthion by e.g. serial number. Like usb.find(), providing
find_all will return a list of all found devices.

Throws a DeviceNotFoundError if no device is avaiable and find_all is not set.

variables.name: cynthion.Cynthion
documentation: pyflakes

classes.name: cynthion._CynthionSingletonWrapper
documentation:
Convenience function that acts like CynthionSingleton, but also allows Magic:
accessing a property on this object will act as though that property had been
accessed on a result of a CynthionSingleton() call.

That's heckin' unreadable, so in short-- accessing a property on a relevant object
will attempt to 1) call the property on the sanest existing Cynthion object; or
2) create a new Cynthion object, if necessary.

modules.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio
documentation: This file is part of Cynthion

classes.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.Directions
documentation: TODOs:
- XXX: Overhaul the GPIO(Collection) class to be more efficient
- More cleanup to use the GPIOPin model.
- Support ranges of pins from the same port (GPIOPort objects?)
- Implement a release function so if e.g. an I2C device is no longer in use
it releases its pins back to the GPIO pool.

classes.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.__init__
documentation:
Sets up the basic fields for a GPIOProvider.

Parameters:
    name_mappings -- Allows callers to rename the local / fixed GPIO pin names.
        Optional; accepts a dictionary mapping their fixed names to their new names, or
        to None to remove the relevant pin from the list of available pins.

        This allows instantiators to give a given GPIO collection more specific names, or
        to hide them from general API display/usage.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.register_gpio
documentation:
Registers a GPIO pin for later use. Usually only used in board setup.

Args:
    name -- The name for the GPIO, usually expressed as a position on
        a Cynthion header.
    line -- An abstract argument passed to subclass methods that serves
        to identify the pin. Subclasses often use this to store e.g. port and pin
        numbers.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.__register_gpio
documentation:
Registers a GPIO pin for later use. Usually only used in board setup.

Args:
    name -- The name for the GPIO, usually expressed as a position on
        a Cynthion header.
    line -- An abstract argument passed to subclass methods that serves
        to identify the pin. Subclasses often use this to store e.g. port and pin
        numbers.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.mark_pin_as_used
documentation: Marks a pin as used by another peripheral.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.mark_pin_as_unused
documentation: Mark a pin as no longer used by another peripheral.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_available_pins
documentation: Returns a list of available GPIO names.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_pin
documentation:
Returns a GPIOPin object by which a given pin can be controlled.

Args:
    name -- The GPIO name to be used.
    unique -- True if this should fail if a GPIO object for this pin
        already exists.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_port
documentation:
Creates a GPIOPort object that can set multiple pins to a binary value.

Arguments are a list of pin names to conglomerate into a port, MSB first. This may result in a GPIOPort
object, or in a derivative class such as a VirtualGPIOPort, depending on the pin locations.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.release_pin
documentation:
Releases a GPIO pin back to the system for re-use, potentially
not as a GPIO.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.set_up_pin
documentation:
Configure a GPIO line for use as an input or output.  This must be
called before the line can be used by other functions.

Parameters:
    line      -- A unique identifier for the given pin that has meaning to the subclass.
    direction -- Directions.IN (input) or Directions.OUT (output)

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.set_pin_state
documentation:
Set the state of an output line.  The line must have previously been
configured as an output using setup().

Parameters:
    line  -- A unique identifier for the given pin that has meaning to the subclass.
    state -- True sets line high, False sets line low

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.read_pin_state
documentation:
Get the state of an input line.  The line must have previously been
configured as an input using setup().

Args:
    line  -- A unique identifier for the given pin that has meaning to the subclass.

Return:
    bool -- True if line is high, False if line is low

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_pin_direction
documentation:
Gets the direction of a GPIO pin.

Args:
    line  -- A unique identifier for the given pin that has meaning to the subclass.

Return:
    bool -- True if line is an output, False if line is an input

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_pin_port
documentation:
Returns the 'port number' for a given GPIO pin.

For providers for which 'port' isn't a valid semantic concept, this should return
the same identifier for every pin that can be logically written in a single operation.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOProvider.get_pin_identifier
documentation:
Returns the 'pin number' for a given GPIO pin.

This number is typically the 'bit number' in a larger, organized port. For providers
in which this isn't a valid semantic concept, any convenient semantic identifier (or None)
is acceptable.
Base class for an object that provides access to GPIO pins.

classes.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    board -- Cynthion board whose GPIO lines are to be controlled

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.set_up_pin
documentation:
Configure a GPIO line for use as an input or output.  This must be
called before the line can be used by other functions.

Args:
    line -- (port, pin); typically a tuple from J1, J2, J7 below
    direction -- Directions.IN (input) or Directions.OUT (output)

TODO: allow pull-up/pull-down resistors to be configured for inputs

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.set_pin_state
documentation:
Set the state of an output line.  The line must have previously been
configured as an output using setup().

Args:
    line -- (port, pin); typically a tuple from J1, J2, J7 below
    state -- True sets line high, False sets line low

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.read_pin_state
documentation:
Get the state of an input line.  The line must have previously been
configured as an input using setup().

Args:
    line -- (port, pin); typically a tuple from J1, J2, J7 below

Return:
    bool -- True if line is high, False if line is low

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.get_pin_direction
documentation:
Gets the direction of a GPIO pin.

Args:
    line -- (port, pin); typically a tuple from J1, J2, J7 below

Return:
    bool -- True if line is an output, False if line is an input

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.get_pin_port
documentation: Returns the 'port number' for a given GPIO pin.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.get_pin_identifier
documentation: Returns the 'pin number' for a given GPIO pin.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIO.output
documentation: Deprecated methods.
Work with the GPIO directly present on the Cynthion board.

classes.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new object representing a GPIO Pin. Usually instantiated via
a GPIO object.

Args:
    gpio_provider -- The GPIO object to which this pin belongs.
    name -- The name of the given pin. Should match a name registered
        in its GPIO collection.
    line -- The pin's 'line' information, as defined by the object that created
        this GPIO pin. This variable has semantic meaning to the GPIO collection;
        but doesn't have any semantic meaning to this class.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.set_direction
documentation: Sets the GPIO pin to use a given direction.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.get_direction
documentation: Returns the pin's direction; will be either Directions.IN or Directions.OUT

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.is_input
documentation: Convenience function that returns True iff the pin is configured as an input.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.is_output
documentation: Convenience function that returns True iff the pin is configured as an output.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.read
documentation: Convenience alias for get_state.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.input
documentation: Convenience function that sets the pin to an input and reads its value.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.get_state
documentation: Returns the value of a GPIO pin.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.write
documentation: Convenience alias for set_state.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.set_state
documentation:
Write a given value to the GPIO port.

Args:
    high -- True iff the pin should be set to high; the pin will be set
        to low otherwise.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.high
documentation: Convenience function that sets the given GPIO pin to both output mode and high, at once.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.low
documentation: Convenience function that sets the given GPIO pin to both output mode and low, at once.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.get_port
documentation: Returns device's port number, if possible.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.GPIOPin.get_pin
documentation: Returns pin's pin number within its port, if possible.
Class representing a single GPIO pin.

classes.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.__init__
documentation:
Creates a virtual GPIO Port from GPIOPin-compatible objects.

pins -- A list of pins to be coalesced into a virtual port;
    with the MSB first. For convenience, pins (or lists) can
    also be provided as variadic arguments. Pins should already
    have their directions / resistors set.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.set_direction
documentation:
Sets the direction of each individual pin.

Parameters:
    word -- A number whose bits contain 1s for each bit that should be an output,
        and zeroes for each bit that should be an input.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.all_output
documentation:
Sets all of the pins in this port to output mode.

Parameters:
    initial_value -- Optional; the start value to apply to each pin.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.all_input
documentation: Sets all of the pins in this port to output mode.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.read
documentation: Returns the integer value of the relevant port.

methods.name: cynthion.interfaces.gpio.VirtualGPIOPort.write
documentation: Writes a given integer value to the port.
An object that represents a "virtually contiguous" group of GPIO pins.

documentation: Returns the path to the requested asset filename

functions.name: cynthion.commands.util.find_cynthion_bitstream
documentation: Returns the path to the requested bitstream for the appropriate platform

functions.name: cynthion.commands.util.flash_bitstream
documentation: Flashes the given filename to the FPGA configuration flash

functions.name: cynthion.commands.util.flash_mcu_firmware
documentation: Flashes the given filename to the device's Microcontroller

functions.name: cynthion.commands.util.flash_soc_firmware
documentation: Flashes the given filename to the section of configuration flash reserved for the SoC

functions.name: cynthion.commands.util._get_appropriate_platform_name
documentation: Returns the LUNA platform name for the connected Cynthion

methods.name: cynthion.selftest.host.StandaloneTester.assertPhyRegister
documentation:
Asserts that a PHY register contains a given value.

Parameters:
    phy_register_base -- The base address of the PHY window in the debug SPI
                         address range.
    register          -- The PHY register to check.
    value             -- The expected value of the relevant PHY register.

methods.name: cynthion.selftest.host.StandaloneTester.assertPhyReadBack
documentation:
Writes a value to the PHY scratch register and asserts that the read-back matches.

Parameters:
    phy_register_base -- The base address of the PHY window in the debug SPI
                         address range.
    value             -- The value written to the scratch register.

methods.name: cynthion.selftest.host.StandaloneTester.assertPhyPresence
documentation: Assertion that fails iff the given PHY isn't detected.

methods.name: cynthion.selftest.host.StandaloneTester.assertHyperRAMRegister
documentation: Assertion that fails iff a RAM register doesn't hold the expected value.
